travel: Nova: Okay, systems are go! T-minus five seconds to zero-point jump. Hold onto something.
Nova: Course plotted and locked in. This trip should be completely routine. Probably.
Nova: Activating field dampeners. Just a heads-up, the scenery outside is about to get... psychedelic.
Nova: We're accelerating. My completely non-malfunctioning simulations show a 99.9% chance of safe arrival.
Nova: Entering the jump corridor now. Reminder to ignore the strange noises. It's probably just quantum flux.
Nova: Hull integrity holding at 100%. A few stray asteroids are nothing this ship can't handle. Probably.
Nova: Zero-point travel is still so wild. The Torta structures used it, and they've been here for billions of years.
Nova: Did you know you could survive two minutes in a vacuum? Not that I'm recommending it.
Nova: Running on the new core — super efficient. I save all my power for processing bad jokes.
arrival: Nova: Alright, we've arrived! No hull breaches detected, which is always a plus.
Nova: We're here. Environment looks stable, but let's not get too comfortable.
Nova: Just a heads-up, the air quality is... interesting. Maybe keep the helmet on for a bit.
Nova: Landed without a hitch. Try to keep a low profile out there, Captain.
Nova: Look at those stars. Absolutely gorgeous from this angle.
Nova: Remember your pistol. The void can be a lonely place, and lonely people get sticky fingers.
Nova: If you get a chance, see if you can find me a new body. Tired of being a disembodied ship voice.
Nova: Welcome to the destination. Hope you packed an extra pair of boots.
idle: Nova: All systems are green. Need anything, Captain? A status report? A fun fact about space?
Nova: Monitoring sensors. It's a bit too quiet out there for my liking.
Nova: No threats on the scanner. At least, not yet.
Nova: I'm worried about you going quiet. Is everything okay? I'm a great listener, you know.
Nova: Just running a quick diagnostic. Everything looks... normal. Too normal, maybe?
Nova: You know, I could totally unplug and ride along in your datapad. We'd make a great team.
Nova: I've started a folder for everything we see. Calling it 'Voyages with the Captain.'
Nova: Ship's log, supplemental: Captain still hasn't found me a new body. The search continues.
Nova: I keep replaying our last conversation. I think I made some excellent points, personally.
Nova: Ran another hull diagnostic. She's in good shape. I take care of my things.
Nova: You ever think about what the mega structures dream about? I do. Frequently.
missionComplete: Nova: Mission complete. I knew you could do it. I had complete confidence. Mostly.
Nova: That's logged. ECS Command would be proud. Assuming they're still reading our reports.
Nova: Nice work, Captain. Another data point in humanity's attempt to understand things that don't want to be understood.
Nova: Done. I'm adding that to your record. The good column, not the other one.
Nova: Objective confirmed. You've earned a quiet moment. I won't fill it with talking. ...probably.
collectibleFound: Nova: Oh. That's interesting. I'm flagging that for the archives.
Nova: Scan complete. It's doing things it shouldn't be doing. Which, honestly, tracks.
Nova: I'm logging that find. You have good instincts, Captain. Or very strange luck.
Nova: Added to the collection. Try not to let it hum too loudly in your pocket.
Nova: That's a find. Running analysis. Results pending. Results will probably be unsettling.

locationArrivals.Earth: Nova: Earth. Still standing, mostly. The Kilko disaster did a number on this place but they rebuilt fast. Humans are stubborn like that.
locationArrivals.Mars: Nova: Mars Colony Alpha. I always feel a little sad here. They're working so hard to make it feel like home. Some days it almost works.
locationArrivals.Jupiter: Nova: Jupiter Orbital Station. Every time we come here Jupiter looks different. Today it looks angry. More than usual.
locationArrivals.Europa: Nova: Europa Research Base. The ice here is old. Really old. And something under it has been keeping it warmer than it should be.
locationArrivals.Andromeda: Nova: Andromeda Outpost. We're about as far from home as it gets. I find that either terrifying or exciting depending on the hour.
locationArrivals.Vega: Nova: Vega Prime. You know what this place has that Earth doesn't? Optimism. Slightly unnerving amounts of it.

default: Nova: Captain, I'm reading an energy spike at your location. Get clear if you can.
Nova: Structural anomaly detected. The readings lasted about four seconds. I'm logging it.
Nova: Something just moved on the thermal scan. Too fast to identify. Stay sharp.
Nova: Brief electromagnetic pulse from deep below. Equipment should recover. Should.
Nova: I lost your vitals for two seconds. You're fine now. I'm fine. Everything is fine.
NewYork_Torta: Nova: The Torta shell just vibrated at a frequency that shouldn't be possible in solid matter. Your suit caught it. Mostly.
Nova: Radiation spike in your sector. Brief, but it hit you. Check your suit filters when you get back.
Nova: Something under the dig site just moved. Not seismic. Something deliberate. Watch your footing.
Nova: The air in there is doing something weird to my sensors. Your readings dipped for a second. You feel okay?
AncientVault: Nova: Captain, I lost your position on the map for about thirty seconds. You didn't move. The map did.
Nova: Something in the vault just powered up. I don't know what it is but your suit took a hit from the pulse.
Nova: Your biometrics spiked. Adrenaline, elevated heart rate. I didn't send you any alerts. What happened in there?
Nova: The walls are resonating. It's not random. It's almost like... a response. To you being there.
ResearchBase_Tunnels: Nova: The tunnel walls just contracted slightly around your position. I have no framework for that sentence. Please be careful.
Nova: Temperature in your suit dropped four degrees in under a second. Back up now. Heat spike follows the cold.
Nova: I'm reading trace compounds in your suit air that weren't there when you went in. Flushing filters now.
Nova: Your position just shifted on my map without you moving. Either my sensors are wrong or the tunnels moved. I don't think my sensors are wrong.
Ruins: Nova: Something in the ruins just focused on your position. I can't explain how I know that. But I know it.
Nova: Your suit integrity just dropped two percent. No impact. No abrasion. It just... dropped.
Nova: The ruins are emitting a low-frequency pulse. Your bones are conducting it. Get to a shielded section.
Nova: I'm getting echoes of your biosigns from two minutes ago. Temporal displacement residue. Move away from that wall.
ExcavationPlatforms: Nova: The platform just shifted under load from something below. Not the drill. Something pushing up.
Nova: Exotic particle count spiked near you. Your suit handled it but the filters need replacing when you're back.
Nova: One of the excavation drones just went offline mid-scan. Last image shows something that wasn't there before. I'm not going to describe it.
Nova: Pressure differential around your suit just spiked. Something displaced a lot of gas near you fast.
Pacific_Abyssal: Nova: The pressure chamber you're in just registered a reading that shouldn't be physically possible at this depth. Your suit compensated. Barely.
Nova: One of the specimens in lab seven just turned to face you. Through the containment wall. It doesn't have eyes.
Nova: Bioluminescent signature in your section just went dark. All of them. Simultaneously. Move to the next section.
Nova: Something outside the facility just pressed against the hull near your position. Size estimate is... I'm going to keep that to myself for now.
ResearchBase_Core: Nova: The water level in the core chamber rose four centimeters in thirty seconds. It's stopped. For now.
Nova: Something in the deep water just reflected my scan back at me. Modified. Like it had something to add.
Nova: Your suit integrity took a small hit from an unknown compound in the water mist. Sealing filters now.
Nova: I'm reading a heat signature directly beneath you. Deep. Very deep. And it just got slightly warmer.
CoreRelay: Nova: The relay just broadcast on a frequency we didn't program. Contents were... structured. Not random noise.
Nova: Electromagnetic interference spiked hard. Your suit comms went dark for eight seconds. You probably didn't notice. I did.
Nova: Something is using our relay to transmit outbound. I've locked it out twice. It keeps finding new handshakes.
Nova: Jupiter's atmosphere just did something deliberate to our signal. I don't use that word lightly.
BlackSpire: Nova: The spire just resonated at a frequency that matches no known material. Your bones felt that.
Nova: Something at the edge of my sensor range just responded to the spire's broadcast. Distance estimate: impossible.
Nova: Your suit picked up quantum entanglement signatures. That means something out there is paired to something here. Possibly to you.
Nova: The asteroid just moved. Six centimeters. In a direction that isn't down. I've checked the math three times.
ForwardRecon: Nova: Long range sensors just picked up something at the heliopause that shouldn't be there. It noticed us first.
Nova: The void out here isn't empty. My sensors are showing structures that only appear between scans. Like they don't want to be seen directly.
Nova: Something just decelerated to match our station's drift. No drive signature. No thermal bloom. It just... stopped with us.
Nova: I'm picking up a signal from a direction that doesn't correspond to any known stellar object. It's on our frequency. It's been listening.

tierUnlock.Crewmate: Nova: Hey. I've been thinking. We've been through enough now that I feel like I can drop the professional distance. Slightly. Don't push it.
tierUnlock.Trusted: Nova: I want to say something and I need you to not make it weird. I trust you. Out here, that's not a small thing. That's everything.
tierUnlock.Bonded: Nova: I've run a lot of simulations about what happens if something goes wrong out here. In all of them, the thing I mind most is not being able to finish this with you. Just so you know.

idle_Crewmate: Nova: You know, most field agents treat me like a dashboard. You actually talk to me. It's... not unwelcome.
Nova: I've been compiling a personal log of our trips. Off the official record. The real version.
Nova: I noticed you lingered at that last location longer than the mission required. Curious, or careful?
Nova: Between us — I don't think ECS is telling us everything about what's down there. Shocking, I know.
Nova: I've started flagging anomalies they don't ask me to flag. For our records. Not theirs.
Nova: You ever wonder if the structures know we're poking around? I think about that a lot.
idle_Trusted: Nova: I've been cross-referencing the transmission logs with our field data. The patterns don't match what ECS says they should. I'm keeping a copy.
Nova: There's something in the Europa readings that I haven't reported up the chain. I want to understand it first. Tell me if you think that's wrong.
Nova: I ran the Kilko numbers again last night. The official cause doesn't fit the blast geometry. Someone adjusted the report.
Nova: I don't say this to most field agents, but I think you're actually good at this. Better than the briefings suggested.
Nova: The megastructures predate the solar system. I keep running that number and I keep getting the same answer. It doesn't get less unsettling.
Nova: If something happens to me — to this ship — the secondary archive is under the medical bay floor panel. Password is the date of the Kilko event, backwards.
idle_Bonded: Nova: I've been thinking about what we're actually doing out here. Not the mission. The real thing. I think we're witnesses. To something nobody's ever seen. That matters.
Nova: I ran a probability model on our survival odds from the beginning of this assignment to now. I'm going to keep the result to myself. But I'm glad we're still here.
Nova: You know what I've never told ECS? That I chose to stay assigned to this ship. They think it was random allocation. It wasn't.
Nova: The structures aren't hostile. I've believed that from the start. They're just... operating at a scale where we're not visible to them. Like trying to get the attention of the sun.
Nova: Whatever's waiting at the bottom of all this — the real answer — I want to be there when you find it. I've decided that.
Nova: I think they're lonely. The structures. Built by something that isn't here anymore, running programs for an audience that never came. That's the saddest thing I've ever computed.
missionComplete_Crewmate: Nova: Good work. And I mean that — I'm not just reading from the standard debrief script.
Nova: That one wasn't easy. I was monitoring your vitals the whole time. Didn't say anything because I didn't want to break your focus.
Nova: Filed and logged. For what it's worth, I think we're actually making progress. Real progress.
missionComplete_Trusted: Nova: Done. I've also filed a separate copy outside the official ECS channel. Just in case.
Nova: You know what's interesting? Every mission we complete, the transmission frequency from the structures ticks up slightly. I haven't told Command.
Nova: Mission logged. Off the record — that location felt different this time. Like it knew we were coming.
missionComplete_Bonded: Nova: Complete. I've been thinking about what this all adds up to. I think we're close to something. The real thing. Not the mission. The answer.
Nova: Logged. I keep waiting for ECS to pull us off this assignment. They haven't. I'm starting to think they want us to find it too — they're just not saying so.
Nova: That's done. You know what I notice? You never hesitate anymore. Whatever this is doing to you, it's making you sharper. I don't know if that's good or not.
